channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Ancient Excel bug comes out of retirement for active attacks
item.description: A 17-year-old critical Excel vulnerability has resurfaced, prompting CISA to issue an alert as it is now being actively exploited. This highlights ongoing security risks in widely used software.

item.title: Raspberry Pi OS ends open-door policy for sudo
item.description: The latest Raspberry Pi OS update now requires a password for the sudo command by default, enhancing security for users. This change aims to prevent unauthorized access and improve system integrity.

item.title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
item.description: Cybercriminals are using illicit tools sold on Telegram to bypass bank security measures, showcasing a troubling trend in financial fraud. This highlights the evolving tactics of scammers in the digital age.

item.title: Fission impossible: Uncle Sam wants nuclear reactors in space by 2031
item.description: The US government aims to deploy nuclear reactors in space by 2031, with plans for some on the Moon and in orbit. This ambitious initiative reflects a significant step in space exploration and energy generation.

item.title: The best internal communication tools of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best internal communication tools for remote work in 2026 highlights the importance of reliable platforms for effective collaboration. This is crucial for maintaining productivity in distributed teams.

item.title: AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy
item.description: A new study reveals that AI-generated websites are contributing to a misleadingly positive online atmosphere, raising concerns about authenticity and trust on the internet. This trend could impact user perceptions and interactions.

item.title: UK told its Big Tech habit is now a national security risk
item.description: A report warns that the UK's reliance on US Big Tech has become a national security risk, exposing vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure. This calls for a reevaluation of technology dependencies.

item.title: The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought
item.description: An analysis found that nearly 90 schools and 600 students globally have been affected by AI-generated deepfake nude images, highlighting a growing crisis in educational environments. This issue raises serious concerns about privacy and safety.

item.title: Britain's atomic brain trust gives itself till 2030 to unpick fusion challenges
item.description: The UK Atomic Energy Authority has allocated £2.5 billion to tackle fusion power challenges, aiming to achieve significant breakthroughs by 2030. This investment underscores the country's commitment to advancing clean energy technology.

item.title: Meet HoloTab by HCompany. Your AI browser companion.
item.description: HCompany has introduced HoloTab, an AI browser companion designed to enhance user experience while browsing the internet. This innovation aims to streamline online interactions and provide personalized assistance.

item.title: No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
item.description: Synthetic biologists and ethicists discussed the potential creation of 'mirror' bacteria at a conference, raising concerns about the risks involved. This idea could lead to significant advancements in synthetic biology but also poses ethical dilemmas.

item.title: Waymo's self-driving cars face their toughest test yet: London
item.description: Waymo has begun testing its self-driving cars in London with human operators ready to intervene. This marks a significant step in the company's efforts to expand its autonomous ride-hailing services internationally.

item.title: Agents hooked into GitHub can steal creds – but Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft haven't warned users
item.description: Security researchers discovered vulnerabilities in AI agents connected to GitHub, allowing them to steal sensitive credentials. Despite the risks, major companies like Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft have not alerted users about these flaws.

item.title: Half of all US employees use AI at work now - and waste almost 8 hours a week doing it
item.description: A Gallup report shows that half of US employees are using AI at work, but many are wasting nearly eight hours a week on ineffective AI interactions. This indicates both the growing reliance on AI and the need for better integration.

item.title: The latest Google Home update brings Gemini fixes that I'm actually excited to try again
item.description: The latest update for Google Home enhances the Gemini experience with various fixes and improvements. Users can expect a more reliable performance from the device following this update.

item.title: Grok’s sexual deepfakes almost got it banned from Apple’s App Store. Almost.
item.description: Apple nearly banned Elon Musk's AI app, Grok, due to its inability to manage nonconsensual sexual deepfakes. This incident underscores the challenges of content moderation in AI applications.

item.title: Don’t miss Acer’s 14-inch touchscreen Chromebook with MediaTek Kompanio Ultra while it’s $200 off
item.description: Best Buy is offering a $200 discount on Acer's Chromebook Spin 514 during its Ultimate Upgrade sale. This promotion makes the device more accessible to consumers looking for a touchscreen Chromebook.

item.title: AI hiring humans? Startup tests real-time retail agent in San Francisco
item.description: A startup is testing an AI agent named Luna in a San Francisco retail store, managing hiring and operations. This represents a new phase in AI's role in the workforce.

item.title: Show HN: Host, share, promote and track release artifacts via your coding agent
item.description: A new coding agent aims to streamline the management of release artifacts for developers, allowing for efficient command execution directly from the terminal. This could enhance productivity in software development.

item.title: Time, Cosmos, Reform, and Redemption: Calendars in the Light of History and Theology
item.description: An article discusses the significance of calendars in relation to history and theology, particularly in the context of the Easter season. It reflects on how timekeeping influences cultural and religious practices.

item.title: Oracle Corporation (ORCL) Poised for $14B Financing for Data Centers Development
item.description: Oracle Corporation is set to secure $14 billion in financing for its data center development, indicating strong growth potential. This move reflects investor confidence in Oracle's future in cloud computing.

item.title: Apple Watch Ultra 3 and Series 11 are up to $100 off this week with deals from $299
item.description: Apple is offering discounts of up to $100 on the Apple Watch Ultra 3 and Series 11, making these premium devices more accessible. This promotion reflects Apple's strategy to boost sales amid competitive pressures.

item.title: Google and Anthropic Deal Strengthen Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Growth Prospects
item.description: Google and Anthropic's partnership is expected to enhance Broadcom's growth prospects, particularly in AI and cloud services. This collaboration signals a strategic move to leverage AI advancements.

item.title: Tuesday: Hili dialogue
item.description: This article discusses International Laverbread Day, a quirky celebration of a traditional Welsh food made from seaweed. It highlights cultural food practices but lacks relevance to tech or AI.

item.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Cloud Unit AWS Inks Deals with Uber and Coupa
item.description: Amazon Web Services has secured new deals with Uber and Coupa, reinforcing its position in the cloud market. These partnerships are crucial for AWS's growth and service expansion.

item.title: A new Gemini feature could be coming to help you plan your day
item.description: Google is developing a new feature for its Gemini platform called 'Your Day,' aimed at helping users manage their daily schedules. This feature may integrate data from various Google apps.

item.title: Exclusive: AlphaSense names new CFO as revenue tops $500 million
item.description: AlphaSense has appointed Samantha Greenberg as its new CFO as the company reports revenue exceeding $500 million. This leadership change may influence its strategic direction in the AI market.

item.title: Kraken Extortion Plot Explodes — Should Traders Continue To Trust Exchanges With Their Personal Data?
item.description: An extortion plot involving Kraken has surfaced, with claims of access to client account information following insider incidents. This raises concerns about data security in cryptocurrency exchanges.

item.title: Morning Minute: The SEC Just Gave DeFi The Green Light
item.description: The SEC's recent actions have positively impacted the DeFi sector, leading to price increases in Bitcoin and Ethereum. This development could signal a more favorable regulatory environment for decentralized finance.

item.title: WATCH: Pilot speaks out after crash landing
item.description: A small plane made an emergency landing on a busy street in Phoenix without serious injuries reported. The incident highlights the importance of aviation safety and emergency preparedness.

item.title: mcp-video-preview added to PyPI
item.description: MCP video preview has been added to PyPI, allowing users to visually inspect video files, extract frames, and check overlays. This tool enhances video analysis capabilities for developers.

item.title: 10 Reasons to Wait for the iPhone 18 Pro
item.description: Rumors suggest that Apple is developing the iPhone 18 Pro with several advanced features, prompting speculation about its potential impact on the smartphone market. This ongoing innovation reflects Apple's commitment to staying ahead in technology.

item.title: Assassination attempt targets Jewish OpenAI CEO
item.description: An assassination attempt on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman involved a suspect throwing a Molotov cocktail at his home, motivated by fears about AI's impact. This incident underscores the growing tensions surrounding AI development.

item.title: The Morning After: Meta is reportedly working on an AI model of Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: Meta is reportedly developing an AI model of CEO Mark Zuckerberg, aiming to replicate his mannerisms and tone. This project has sparked criticism and concerns about the implications of such AI representations.

item.title: How To Break Through An Affiliate Site Plateau & Find New Growth – Ask An SEO via @sejournal, @rollerblader
item.description: This article provides strategies for breaking through affiliate marketing plateaus using data analysis and audience research. It offers actionable insights for marketers seeking growth.

item.title: Garmin releases new ultra-premium smartwatch with cellular and satellite connectivity
item.description: Garmin has launched a new ultra-premium smartwatch featuring cellular and satellite connectivity, boasting a bright AMOLED display and long battery life. This release targets high-end consumers looking for advanced features.

item.title: How China's Sulphuric Acid Ban Will Hit Metals & Fertiliser
item.description: China's ban on sulphuric acid exports is expected to disrupt global supply chains for metals and fertilizers, exacerbating procurement challenges. This move highlights geopolitical tensions affecting the economy.

item.title: Gemma 4 : Google’s New Open-Source Local AI That Requires No Internet
item.description: Google has introduced Gemma 4, an open-source AI model that operates offline, capable of processing various media types. This innovation allows users to utilize AI without internet dependency.

item.title: Inspectorio AI platform to enhance Gap supply chain oversight
item.description: Inspectorio's AI platform aims to improve supply chain oversight for Gap, enhancing transparency and efficiency in operations. This development reflects the growing importance of AI in retail logistics.

item.title: torch-aac 0.2.0
item.description: The torch-aac 0.2.0 release features a GPU-accelerated AAC-LC encoder using PyTorch, enhancing audio processing capabilities for developers. This update is significant for those working with audio applications.

item.title: While Canadians Are Open to Embracing AI in Their Homes, Workplace, and Even Between the Sheets, H&R Block Survey Points to Cautionary Tale That ChatGPT Is Not Your Friend for Tax Filing
item.description: A survey reveals Canadians are cautious about using AI tools like ChatGPT for tax filing, highlighting concerns over security and accuracy in personal finances. While they embrace AI in various aspects of life, the risks in sensitive areas like taxes remain a significant concern.

item.title: Brand new M4 iPad Air up to $100 off with deals starting from $551
item.description: Amazon is offering discounts of up to $100 on the new M4 iPad Air, with various configurations now available at all-time low prices. This promotion highlights Apple's continued push in the tablet market amidst competitive pricing.

item.title: From farms to labs, Indonesia bets on better seeds to boost food security
item.description: Indonesia is investing in improved seed technology to enhance food security, aiming to boost agricultural productivity. This initiative reflects a strategic approach to address future food demands and sustainability.

item.title: ConnectureDRX and HealthcareGPS® Launch MedicareCopilot™
item.description: HealthcareGPS® and ConnectureDRX have launched MedicareCopilot™, an AI platform designed for Medicare agents to efficiently compare plans and enroll clients. This tool aims to streamline the enrollment process and improve service delivery.

item.title: AI is changing cloud operations, but not in the way orgs expect
item.description: Organizations are shifting focus from deploying AI to ensuring its reliable and safe operation in alignment with business goals. This change underscores the complexities involved in managing AI systems at scale.

item.title: commit-defender added to PyPI
item.description: The commit-defender tool has been added to PyPI, providing AI-powered pre-commit code reviews to catch issues before they are integrated into codebases. This tool aims to enhance code quality and developer productivity.

item.title: Department for Transport shows how its AI system avoids bias
item.description: A report from the Department for Transport showcases an AI system designed to extract themes from public consultations, emphasizing checks to avoid bias. This highlights the importance of accountability in AI applications.

item.title: causabi-geo 0.1.2
item.description: The causabi-geo 0.1.2 update improves how AI search engines interpret websites, enhancing visibility and search optimization. This update is crucial for web developers looking to improve their site's performance.

item.title: hf 1.10.2
item.description: The hf 1.10.2 update introduces a command-line interface extracted from the huggingface_hub library, facilitating interaction with the Hugging Face Hub. This update aims to streamline user experience for developers.

item.title: What the Rumored Fable Delay Means for PS5 Players in 2026
item.description: The release of the Fable reboot by Playground Games has been delayed to spring 2027, shifting from the previously expected late 2026 launch. This delay reflects internal challenges faced during development.
